title: Taking Turns
lesson: Waiting and taking turns means everyone gets a chance to play.
prompts: In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits on the swing seat, both hands gripping the two chains and legs stretched forward as they swing, beaming happily toward two approaching friends. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06} walk up across the grass toward the swing, each raising one open hand to wave hello, smiling warmly at the person from the input image. Make the interaction read physically: the friends' gazes and waves point toward the swinging child, who looks back toward them. Exactly one child, one girl, and one boy in the frame, and no other people. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits on the swing seat gripping the two chains, leaning back with a surprised expression. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06} crowd in on either side of the occupied swing, each grabbing one chain with one hand and leaning in eagerly to get a turn. Make the interaction read physically: both friends' hands clutch the chains of the one swing the child is sitting on, their gazes fixed on the swing. Exactly one child, one girl, and one boy in the frame, and no other people. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands at the left, turned to the right toward two friends, one open hand raised gently at shoulder height as if suggesting they wait their turn, with a calm, friendly smile. To the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06} stand side by side back from the swing, both turned to face the person from the input image, each making a small open-hand conversational gesture, with patient, attentive expressions. Make the interaction read physically: the child's raised open hand and gaze aim toward the girl and boy, while the girl's and boy's gazes and open-hand gestures aim back toward the child. Each is a single distinct child — no fourth child, no duplicate or extra child, no twin. Exactly one child, one girl, and one boy in the frame, and no other people. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands to one side clapping both hands together, with a cheerful, encouraging expression. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} sits on the swing seat gripping the two chains, legs stretched forward as she swings with a delighted grin, while {GENDER_M_AGE_06} stands close behind the swing with both hands pressed gently against her back to push her, with an excited smile. Make the interaction read physically: the boy's hands touch the girl's back and his gaze follows the swing, while the person from the input image looks toward the swinging girl. Exactly one child, one girl, and one boy in the frame, and no other people. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits on the swing seat, both hands gripping the two chains and legs stretched forward as they swing up, beaming with a joyful smile. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} stands close behind the swing with both hands pressed gently against the swinging child's back to push, with an encouraging smile, while {GENDER_M_AGE_06} stands to one side waiting his turn, clapping and cheering with a delighted expression. Make the interaction read physically: the girl's hands touch the swinging child's back and her gaze follows the swing, while the boy looks toward the swinging child. Exactly one child, one girl, and one boy in the frame, and no other people. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — three children stand in one straight left-to-right row, evenly spaced with a small gap of grass between neighbors and joined only by their clasped hands, all laughing with big happy smiles. From left to right: first {GENDER_F_AGE_06}, then the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image in the middle, then {GENDER_M_AGE_06}, each holding the next child's hand. Each is a single distinct child standing apart as its own separate figure — exactly one girl and not two, no fourth child, no duplicate or extra child, no twin. Exactly one child, one girl, and one boy in the frame, and no other people. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}
negative_prompts: two children sitting on one swing, a second child seated on the swing seat, a friend sitting on the child's lap, extra swing, second swing, multiple swings
fourth child, extra child, duplicate child, twin, more than three children, extra person in the background, crowd of children
fourth child, extra child, duplicate child, twin, second girl, two girls, more than three children, extra person in the background, crowd of children
gists: At a sunny playground, the child swings happily on the single swing while two friends, a girl and a boy, walk up and wave hello — the child is enjoying the swing.
By the swing, the child sits on the swing looking surprised as the two friends grab its chains from either side, each wanting the one swing at once — everyone wants the same swing.
By the swing, the child raises one hand calmly suggesting they wait their turn while the two friends stand back and discuss the idea with patient, attentive expressions.
By the swing, the child claps and cheers as the girl swings with a delighted grin and the boy gently pushes her from behind — a friend takes the first turn.
By the swing, the child swings with a joyful smile while the girl gently pushes from behind and the boy waits beside cheering — it's the child's turn, and the friends help and wait.
By the swing, the child and the two friends hold hands in a row, all happy — everyone got a turn and had fun.
texts: {NAME} was having a wonderful time on the swing — up and down, up and down! Two friends came over. "Hi, {NAME}!" they called, waving. "Watch me go!" laughed {NAME}.
But the friends wanted the swing too. "My turn now!" said the girl, grabbing a chain. "No, me first!" said the boy, grabbing the other. The swing wobbled to a stop. "Wait!" said {NAME}.
"Let's take turns," said {NAME} gently, raising a hand. "Good idea," said the girl, and the friends stepped back and nodded.
The girl went first — wheee! "Higher, higher!" cheered {NAME}, clapping, while the boy gave a gentle push. "This is so much fun!" laughed the girl.
Then it was {NAME}'s turn! The girl gave a gentle push. "Wheee!" laughed {NAME}, swinging up high. "You go so high!" cheered the boy, waiting beside for his turn.
Everyone got a turn, and everyone had fun. "That was the best!" said {NAME}. They all held hands, as happy as can be. Taking turns means everyone gets to play.
